PS-BGIT-002.author: m.berger
PS-BGIT-002.text: How do we surface to the client that their application is parked at credit? Today the silence is the biggest CX failure. Worth quantifying the average wait at credit before we approve this step as final.

PS-BGIT-002.author: Process Specialist
PS-BGIT-002.text:
Process Specialist — comment review on PS-BGIT-002 (Credit and Facility Check)

Incorporated (1 comment, 2 changes):

1. Added `sla` field: "Credit team response: informal 1–2 business days; no formal SLA — see PG-BGIT-001". The absence of a formal SLA was already documented as an open gap (PG-BGIT-001) but was not visible on the step itself. It is now surfaced directly.

2. Extended "What happens" with a second paragraph naming the two documented weaknesses: (a) no proactive client notification while the application is parked at credit, cross-referenced to FP-BGIT-002; (b) no formal Credit team response SLA, cross-referenced to PG-BGIT-001. The first paragraph was tightened to preserve the 95-word block limit.

Element reverted to in-progress — requires SME re-approval once the "What happens" provenance is confirmed.

Linked elements checked for drift: FP-BGIT-002 (friction point — no credit notification) and PG-BGIT-001 (process gap — no Credit SLA) are now explicitly cited in the step text, which is consistent with their existing content. DEP-BGIT-003 (Credit team upstream dependency) is also consistent. No linked element is contradicted by these changes.

CP-BGIT-001.author: m.berger
CP-BGIT-001.text: This control's owner is the same role that does the work (TFM does Step 5 and approves Step 5). Is that genuinely four-eyes, or does the documented four-eyes only kick in for >EUR 5m via Head of Trade Finance? Please clarify the control description.

TS-BGIT-001.author: m.berger
TS-BGIT-001.text: What's the rollback path if the AI completeness validator rejects an application a human would accept? We can't have AI false-positives blocking legitimate intake — need an override + audit-trail by the TFO.
